meta.title: Lake Mead Operating Tier for 2027
meta.question: What operating tier will USBR's August 2026 24-Month Study trigger for 2027 Colorado River operations, based on projected Lake Mead January 1 2027 elevation?
meta.resolution: USBR August 2026 24-Month Study release determining 2027 operating tier (Tier 0/1/2/3)

priorJustifications.H1: Tier 0 (≥1,090 ft) requires ~28-ft net rise from current ~1,062 ft in one water year — Probable-Maximum (10th-percentile wet) territory only.
priorJustifications.H2: Tier 1 (1,075-1,089 ft) requires wetter-than-median trace from current ~1,062 ft. USBR Most-Probable forecast for EOY 2026 sits 1,038-1,049 ft (below H2 floor).
priorJustifications.H3: Tier 2 (1,025-1,074 ft) is USBR Most-Probable for Jan 1 2027. CRSS ensemble central trace lands here.
priorJustifications.H4: Tier 3 (<1,025 ft) requires Probable-Minimum (10th-percentile dry) trace. Snowpack largely locked in by April-May, so most of the ensemble dispersion is captured.

meta.dataAnchor: USBR Lower Colorado 24-Month Study Most-Probable + CRSS 5-year probabilistic ensemble (Jan 2026 + April 2026 releases).

rev4_governance_notes.resolution_trigger_note: RESOLUTION TRIGGER: USBR August 2026 24-Month Study tier determination. The April 2026 24-Month Study (t2_april_2026_24mo_study_modal_h{2,3,4}) is a LEADING indicator only — autocorr ~0.85 with August Study but NOT itself the resolution. REV4 demoted these from tier1 to tier2 to reflect this. Resolution flows through update_posteriors when August Study publishes.
rev4_governance_notes.compound_projection_note: Lint compound projection bounds H3 at 0.890 firing all 12 indicators on starting prior. This is conservative — does not model per_event_member exclusion on the April Study triplet, nor causal_event_id de-correlation across wy2026_basin_hydrology cluster. Real-world update sequences expected to stay below 0.85. Engine's saturation_redteam_required gate will trigger if approached.

redTeamGapsAddressed: April→August 24-Month Study revision base rate (±30-40 ft observed)
H4 (sub-1025) physical pathway anchored: 47ft max single-year drop in 2011 + Section 6E release reduction enables H4
WY2025 actual conservation delivered ~613 KAF Lower Basin = 60-70% of pledged
May-July 2026 outlook: ENSO transition to El Niño, weaker SW monsoon, continued dry
Post-2026 DEIS 5 alternatives in review; August Study uses current 2007 Guidelines
meta.priorRevisionRationale: Round 2 confirmed: H4 has real physical pathway (47ft single-year precedent + Section 6E release reduction); ENSO transition to El Niño reduces summer monsoon recovery hope; April→August revisions historically ±30-40 ft. Net: H4 up 5pp (real pathway anchored), H3 down 3pp (April projection more anchored to current 1055), H2 down 2pp.
meta.lensSetReason: Initial lens set during topic creation per skills/news-scan.md mapping

issues: Unfalsifiable hypotheses: H1, H2, H3, H4

blockers: NON-UNIFORM PRIORS without justification — posteriors are {'H1': 0.02, 'H2': 0.13, 'H3': 0.5, 'H4': 0.35} but posteriorHistory has no initial note explaining the asymmetry. Add a posteriorHistory entry documenting why.
warnings: meta.resolution may not be objectively observable — does it describe something a third party could verify?
No tier-1 (critical) indicators — what would it take to near-certainly resolve this question?
No anti-indicators defined — every hypothesis needs at least one indicator that would REDUCE its probability

_design_rules: Each indicator must be OBSERVABLE — actions, not opinions
Each must have a clear fired/not-fired threshold
Every hypothesis needs at least one pro AND one anti indicator
Governor flags unfalsifiable hypotheses as DEGRADED

tier2_strong.desc: USBR April 2026 24-Month Study Most-Probable trace projects Lake Mead Jan 1 2027 elevation in 1075-1089 ft band (Tier 1) — leading indicator vs August 2026 Study which is actual resolution.
USBR April 2026 24-Month Study Most-Probable trace projects Lake Mead Jan 1 2027 elevation in 1025-1074 ft band (Tier 2).
USBR April 2026 24-Month Study Most-Probable trace projects Lake Mead Jan 1 2027 elevation <1025 ft (Tier 3).
Upper Colorado Basin SNOTEL April 1 2026 SWE >=110% of 1991-2020 median (wet trace). Source: NRCS USDA.
Upper Colorado Basin SNOTEL April 1 2026 SWE <=85% of 1991-2020 median (dry trace). Source: NRCS.

USBR Q1 2026 monthly Powell releases (Jan/Feb/Mar) average within 7.5-8.0 MAF annualized rate (modal range — observable in real time during firing window, NOT the AOP-locked annual figure). Source: USBR monthly operations.
DOI invokes Section 5 (shortage extraordinary) language before Aug 2026 study (DATA tag — formal regulatory action, strong LR). Source: DOI Federal Register / USBR press release.

tier3_suggestive.desc: USBR/CBRFC April 2026 unregulated April-July inflow to Powell forecast >=95% of 30-year median. Source: USBR/CBRFC.
USBR/CBRFC April 2026 unregulated April-July inflow to Powell forecast <=70% of 30-year median. Source: USBR/CBRFC.
Lower Basin states (AZ/NV/CA) issue joint emergency conservation statement before Aug 2026 study (RHETORIC tag — political signal of stress, weak LR). Source: state press releases.
NOAA/CPC officially classifies Q1 2026 Upper Basin precipitation as >=130% of normal (well-above-normal trace). Source: NOAA CPC / Western Regional Climate Center.

anti_indicators.desc: CA + AZ jointly forbear >=500 KAF beyond DCP/2007-Guidelines obligations in WY 2026 (demand-side relief pushes elevation toward H1/H2 via reduced delivery, orthogonal to hydrology cluster). Source: USBR + state water authority filings.

actorModel.description: Lake Mead 2027 operating tier set by USBR August 2026 24-Month Study based on Jan 1 2027 elevation projection. Inputs: Upper Basin snowpack/runoff, Powell releases, Lower Basin demand, and any DOI/state-level emergency action.

usbr.name: US Bureau of Reclamation
usbr.role: Operates the system; publishes 24-Month Study; enforces tier determinations.
usbr.decisionStyle: Methodical, schedule-driven, conservative on new operating arrangements.
biases: Risk-averse on tier changes that require state buy-in
filters: SNOTEL snowpack, CBRFC runoff forecast, reservoir storage telemetry
overrides: Section 5 invocation by DOI overrides standard guidelines

lower_basin_states.name: Lower Basin States (CA, AZ, NV)
lower_basin_states.role: Largest delivery cuts under tier escalation. Joint emergency conservation action can shift trajectory directly via demand reduction.
lower_basin_states.decisionStyle: Inter-state bargaining; CA holds senior priority but absorbs cuts under DCP+; AZ takes deepest cuts first.
biases: AZ political tolerance for further cuts is limited
filters: Reservoir levels, agricultural water demand, urban supply security
overrides: Federal Section 5 action overrides state negotiating posture

upper_basin_states.name: Upper Basin States (CO, UT, WY, NM)
upper_basin_states.role: Provide inflows; constrained by 1922 Compact obligations to deliver to Lee Ferry. Demand Management Program is key lever.
upper_basin_states.decisionStyle: Compact-rights protective; resistant to formalized DM that limits future development.
biases: Suspicious of Lower Basin demand expansion
filters: Snowpack, Powell release schedule, demand management proposals

doi.name: Department of Interior (Secretary)
doi.role: Federal trustee. Section 5 (shortage extraordinary) invocation is the nuclear option that overrides state negotiations.
doi.decisionStyle: Politically constrained; reluctant to invoke Section 5 absent acute crisis.
biases: Anchored to negotiated solutions; Section 5 carries litigation risk
filters: Reservoir trajectory, state-coalition coherence, Congressional pressure

noaa_cbrfc.name: NOAA Colorado Basin River Forecast Center
noaa_cbrfc.role: Hydrology forecasting. April runoff forecast feeds USBR Study. CPC seasonal classifications drive expectations.
noaa_cbrfc.decisionStyle: Ensemble-based, methodologically conservative; revises forecasts as Q1 data accumulates.
biases: Anchored to recent climatology baseline (1991-2020 — drier than long-run)
filters: SNOTEL data, soil moisture, antecedent conditions

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC — Joint state emergency statement is RHETORIC (weak LR); DOI Section 5 invocation is EVENT (strong LR). REV4 split these.

RESOLUTION TRIGGER — August 2026 24-Month Study is the resolution event. April Study is leading-indicator only (autocorr ~0.85). Process resolution via update_posteriors (see meta.rev4_governance_notes.resolution_trigger_note).
HYDROLOGY CLUSTER DE-CORRELATION — Snowpack, runoff forecast, and atmospheric river classification all share wy2026_basin_hydrology causal_event_id. Engine collapses joint firings to one update.
DEMAND-SIDE INDEPENDENCE — anti_h3_lower_basin_demand_collapse fires on state forbearance, orthogonal to hydrology. Provides H1/H2 path even in dry trace.
USBR SOURCE CONCENTRATION — 6 of 12 indicators trace to USBR publications. Methodology change is correlated risk; flagged in source_calibration.

usbr_24mo_study.label: USBR 24-Month Study (Most-Probable trace + min/max probable)
usbr_24mo_study.cadence: monthly (most months) — KEY: April + August traces
usbr_24mo_study.note: April 2026 Study fires per_event_member triplet. AUGUST 2026 Study is RESOLUTION TRIGGER (see meta.rev4_governance_notes.resolution_trigger_note) — process via update_posteriors.

usbr_powell_operations.label: USBR monthly Powell release operations
usbr_powell_operations.note: t2_powell_monthly_release_pattern_q1_2026 reads Q1 2026 monthly releases. REV4 dropped stale 'AOP-locked annual figure' indicator.

nrcs_snotel.label: NRCS USDA SNOTEL snowpack (Snow Water Equivalent)
nrcs_snotel.note: April 1 SWE vs 1991-2020 median. Shares causal_event_id=wy2026_basin_hydrology with runoff + AR.

cbrfc_runoff_forecast.label: USBR/CBRFC unregulated April-July inflow to Powell forecast
cbrfc_runoff_forecast.source: Colorado Basin River Forecast Center (NOAA NWS)
cbrfc_runoff_forecast.note: April forecast vs 30-year median. ESP ensemble. Shares wy2026_basin_hydrology causal_event_id.

noaa_cpc_classification.label: NOAA Climate Prediction Center seasonal precipitation classification
noaa_cpc_classification.source: NOAA CPC + Western Regional Climate Center
noaa_cpc_classification.note: Q1 2026 Upper Basin >=130% of normal classification fires t3_atmospheric_river_winter_2026 (REV4: relabeled from fake-anti to honest pro-H1).

doi_section5_invocations.label: DOI Section 5 (shortage extraordinary) invocations + Federal Register

lower_basin_state_statements.label: AZ/NV/CA state water authority joint emergency statements
lower_basin_state_statements.source: ADWR + SNWA + CRWUA + state water authorities
lower_basin_state_statements.note: t3_basin_states_joint_statement (RHETORIC tag, weak LR — REV4 split from Section 5). anti_h3_lower_basin_demand_collapse reads voluntary forbearance >=500 KAF.

evidenceLog.text: Lake Mead water level dropped more than 6 feet since March 1, 2026
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Single data point does not meet Tier 2 indicator threshold; informs context for t2_april_2026_24mo_study_modal_h3.

evidenceLog.text: Lake Mead level steady, but 2-year outlook shows trouble in 2027 — Discusses 24-Month Study projections showing Lake Mead trending toward elevations below the Tier 1 band (1,075-1,089 ft) in 2027, contradicting H2.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)

evidenceLog.text: Lake Mead expected to hit new low in May 2027 — Reports most-probable elevation of ~1,041 ft in May 2027, well below the Tier 1 band, contradicting H2 (Tier 1) and supporting a deeper tier.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)

evidenceLog.text: April 2026 Probable Minimum 24-Month Study — Probable-minimum scenario shows Mead approaching ~1,026 ft, near the Tier 3 boundary (<1,025 ft); bears on H3 by indicating downside risk that could push past Tier 2 into deeper cuts.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)

evidenceLog.text: Latest projections stress the need for robust operational guidelines — Official USBR communication on projections and post-2026 operational decisions affecting the 2027 tier.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)

evidenceLog.text: Lake Mead drops more than 6 feet since March, nearing record low — Recent observed elevation decline informs January 2027 projection inputs to the August 2026 24-Month Study.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)

evidenceLog.text: Lower Colorado Weekly Hydrologic Update: April 27, 2026 — Most recent official hydrologic conditions feeding the 24-Month Study elevation forecast.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)

evidenceLog.text: 20-foot drop: Why more decline is on deck for Lake Mead in 2026 — Discusses reduced Powell-to-Mead releases that directly drive lower projected January 2027 Mead elevation and tier outcome.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)

evidenceLog.text: New plan will boost Lake Powell, take water away from Lake Mead during dry year — Reduced Powell release (7.48 to 6.0 maf) materially lowers projected Mead January 2027 elevation, shifting tier likelihood.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)

evidenceLog.text: Bureau of Reclamation announces actions to protect Colorado River infrastructure — Announces operational interventions affecting Mead inflows and thus the 2027 tier determination.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)

evidenceLog.text: Nevada, California and Arizona offer cuts to Colorado River allocations in emergency plan — Lower Basin emergency cut proposal could alter operating assumptions and tier outcomes for 2027 operations.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
